board.designation: DB-UPS — UPS-backed critical loads sub-DB (10 kVA online double-conversion), IEC commercial 3-storey office

incoming_supply.fed_from: MSB-MAIN F07 (50A MCB Type C, 40m feeder) → 10 kVA online double-conversion UPS → DB-UPS (UPS-output side)

selectivity_results.code_clause: IEC 62040-1:2017 Annex E + IEC 60898 — 50A Type C MCB upstream cascading via the UPS to 6-32A Type C MCBs at DB-UPS. UPS inverter limits short-circuit contribution on UPS-output side to ≈6.5 kA. Worst-case ratio at U01/U06 (32A) = 50:32 = 1.56:1 — BELOW the 2:1 IEC 60898 same-curve cascade threshold. Cascade-critical: under downstream U01 server-rack fault both UPS internal protection AND upstream MSB-MAIN F07 may trip simultaneously. Flagged tool_call_pending for fault-level skill to verify at 6.5 kA UPS-output PFC + ride-through behaviour.

non_compliance_flags.message: DB-UPS carries UPS-backed critical loads — IEC 62040-1:2017 governs the UPS unit (online double-conversion topology + 30-min battery autonomy minimum per design intent + Annex E fault-current contribution declared 6.5 kA on UPS-output side). Battery autonomy duration is design-declared at 30 minutes for orderly shutdown of the server rack; AHJ-approved business-continuity duration (which may extend to 60 min, 2h, or longer per local hospital/data-centre/operational-resilience code) must be verified against the UPS commissioning certificate and battery discharge test report at handover.

Cascade ratio at U01 server rack (50:32 = 1.56:1) sits BELOW the 2:1 IEC 60898 same-curve minimum cascade threshold for time-current selectivity. Under a downstream U01 fault on the UPS-output side, the UPS internal protection + MSB-MAIN F07 may trip simultaneously rather than selectively. Defer to fault-level skill for full coordination study at 6.5 kA UPS-output PFC, including UPS ride-through behaviour, bypass-changeover timing, and manufacturer cascade-table confirmation.

assumptions: DB-UPS is the dedicated UPS-backed critical-loads sub-DB — `role: ups_backed_critical_panel` flagged in the board block; supply_class: ups_plus_essential signals to downstream consumers (riser, generator-sizing, fault-level) that this distribution is both UPS-backed AND classified as essential for business continuity.

IEC 62040-1:2017 governs the upstream 10 kVA online double-conversion UPS unit: rectifier (utility AC → DC bus) + battery bank + inverter (DC bus → clean output AC) + static bypass switch. Online double-conversion topology means the load is ALWAYS fed from the inverter; the bypass only engages during a UPS internal fault or maintenance — there is NO transfer time on utility loss because the load was never on utility.

Battery autonomy designed for 30-min ride-through minimum — sufficient for orderly server-rack shutdown via the BMS controller (U05) and graceful workstation-session preservation. AHJ/business-continuity requirement may extend this; verify at handover.

UPS-output side fault level ≈6.5 kA per IEC 62040-1:2017 Annex E typical for a 10 kVA online double-conversion unit — the inverter's IGBT bridge limits short-circuit contribution to ~150-200% of rated output (≈1.5-2× × 14.4A rated = 22-29A continuous, 6.5 kA peak instantaneous via the bypass static switch during fault).

Type B RCD on U01 (server rack) per IEC 60364-5-53:2002+A2:2015 §531.3.3 — server rack contains SMPS-fed PDUs, dual-PSU servers, and storage controllers with rectifier front-ends that can produce smooth DC residual currents. Type A would be blinded; Type B is mandatory.

Type A RCD on U02-U04 (workstations + lab benches) per IEC 60364-4-41 §411.3.3 — 30 mA additional protection for socket circuits ≤32A. Type A (pulsating-DC sensitive) covers Class 1 single-phase workstation PSUs and laboratory benchtop instruments that produce half-wave-rectified leakage current; sufficient because individual workstation/instrument SMPS produce pulsating-DC rather than smooth-DC leakage.

U05 BMS controller is NOT RCD-protected — control circuit on a dedicated MCB. The BMS panel itself contains internal earth-leakage monitoring on its 24V DC field side; the 230V AC supply to the controller cabinet is treated as a dedicated supervisory circuit not subject to socket-circuit personal-shock provisions (no general-purpose sockets downstream).

U06 UPS bypass (maintenance loop) is NOT RCD-protected — IEC 62040-1:2017 §5.2.4 design principle: the bypass path must allow earth-fault current to flow upstream to MSB-MAIN protective devices for proper isolation. An RCD on the bypass would create a parallel earth path that could cause spurious disconnection during a downstream UPS-load earth fault, defeating the bypass-availability purpose at the exact moment maintenance redundancy is needed.

Critical-power distribution practice per IEEE 446-1995 (Emerald Book): UPS-output panels must be physically and electrically distinct from non-UPS distribution; clear labelling required on every circuit-breaker faceplate so a maintainer never accidentally cross-bonds UPS output to utility AC during fault investigation. DB-UPS placard reads 'UPS-OUTPUT — DO NOT BACK-FEED'.

All Type C MCBs (Ia = 10×In) — handles inrush of server-rack PSU energisation cluster (multiple dual-PSU servers powering on simultaneously) + workstation cold-start + lab-bench inrush. Type B would risk nuisance trip during scheduled-maintenance restart of the server rack.

U06 UPS bypass sized 32A Type C / 6mm² — matches U01 server-rack feeder so the bypass loop can carry the full UPS-output load under maintenance changeover. Cable length 2m (within the server/UPS room) keeps voltage drop negligible during bypass operation.

Selectivity flagged as INFO (cascade-critical) — 50:32 ratio at U01/U06 = 1.56:1 sits below the 2:1 IEC 60898 minimum. Fault-level skill must verify against the UPS manufacturer's coordination chart at 6.5 kA UPS-output PFC + utility-side 22.5 kA at MSB-MAIN F07, including ride-through and bypass-changeover sequence.

drawing_notes: DB-UPS located in the ground-floor server/UPS room, co-located with the 10 kVA online double-conversion UPS cabinet and the server rack it primarily feeds. Short 40m feeder run from MSB-MAIN F07 → UPS input; very short (2-8m) runs from UPS output → DB-UPS → server rack and bypass.
Fed from MSB-MAIN F07 (50A MCB Type C) via 40m 16mm² 5-core Cu XLPE submain to the UPS input. UPS output bonded to building TN-S earth; DB-UPS supplied from the UPS output bus.
Online double-conversion topology: load is ALWAYS fed from the inverter — zero transfer time on utility loss. Bypass static switch engages only for UPS internal fault or scheduled maintenance.

6 circuits + 4 spare ways (W7-W10 reserved — typical future expansion includes additional server-rack zones, secondary BMS interface, video-wall/AV-control rack, or addressable PDU/environmental-monitoring sub-feed).

Role: ups_backed_critical_panel — flagged in board block for downstream SLD/riser/fault-level/generator-sizing consumers; supply_class: ups_plus_essential signals dual-classification (UPS-backed AND essential).
Type B RCD on U01 (server rack) per §531.3.3; Type A RCD on U02-U04 (socket circuits ≤32A) per §411.3.3; U05 (BMS) and U06 (UPS bypass) RCD-free by design.
U06 UPS bypass MUST remain RCD-free per IEC 62040-1:2017 §5.2.4 — bypass path requires upstream fault-current flow for proper isolation.
Battery autonomy: 30-min minimum design intent; AHJ-required business-continuity duration must be verified against UPS commissioning certificate + battery discharge test report at handover.
Selectivity at U01/U06 (50:32 = 1.56:1) is cascade-critical — below the 2:1 minimum same-curve threshold; flagged for fault-level skill coordination study.
Placard on DB-UPS faceplate: 'UPS-OUTPUT — DO NOT BACK-FEED' per IEEE 446-1995 critical-power labelling practice.

rationale.chat_summary: DB-UPS: 50A TPN UPS-backed critical sub-DB on UPS-output side, 6 circuits (server rack 6kVA + 2 workstation rings + lab benches + BMS + bypass), fed via 10 kVA online double-conversion UPS per IEC 62040-1:2017 with 30-min ride-through. Type B RCD on server (§531.3.3 DC leakage), Type A on workstations/lab (§411.3.3), U05/U06 RCD-free. INFO flags: AHJ autonomy verification + cascade 50:32=1.56:1 below 2:1 (fault-level skill to confirm).

sections.title: Board Identification
sections.summary: DB-UPS is the dedicated UPS-backed critical-loads sub-DB for the IEC 3-storey commercial office, located in the ground-floor server/UPS room. Role flagged as `ups_backed_critical_panel` and supply_class as `ups_plus_essential` — signals dual-classification (UPS-backed AND essential) so downstream SLD, riser, fault-level, and generator-sizing skills recognise it as a critical-power distribution requiring distinct labelling, segregation, and coordination treatment.

decisions.label: Board role flagged as ups_backed_critical_panel
decisions.summary: Signals UPS-output distribution to all downstream skills (SLD, riser, fault-level, generator-sizing).

decisions.label: supply_class: ups_plus_essential
decisions.summary: Dual-classification flag — both UPS-backed AND essential for business-continuity planning (e.g., generator-backed at the UPS-input side in larger facilities).

decisions.label: Location: ground-floor server/UPS room
decisions.summary: Co-located with the 10 kVA UPS cabinet and the server rack it feeds — keeps UPS-output cabling short (≤8m) and ensures maintenance access to both UPS and panel.

sections.title: Incoming Supply
sections.summary: 50A TPN intake fed from MSB-MAIN F07 (50A MCB Type C) via 40m 16mm² Cu XLPE submain to the input of a 10 kVA online double-conversion UPS, with DB-UPS supplied from the UPS output bus. Online double-conversion means the load is ALWAYS fed from the inverter; bypass engages only for UPS internal fault or scheduled maintenance. UPS-output fault contribution declared 6.5 kA per IEC 62040-1:2017 Annex E (typical for a 10 kVA online unit — IGBT-bridge inverter limits short-circuit current to ~1.5-2× rated, with peak instantaneous via the static bypass switch).

decisions.label: Supply class: ups_plus_essential
decisions.summary: DB-UPS feeds UPS-backed critical loads — dual-classification flags both the UPS-output topology AND the essential-for-business-continuity status.

decisions.label: Online double-conversion UPS topology
decisions.summary: Load is always fed from inverter — zero transfer time on utility loss; bypass only engages for UPS internal fault or maintenance.
decisions.rule: UPS topology selection for critical loads

decisions.label: 30-min ride-through autonomy (design intent)
decisions.summary: Sufficient for orderly server-rack shutdown via BMS (U05) and graceful workstation-session preservation; AHJ may require longer per local code.
decisions.rule: Battery autonomy sizing for orderly shutdown

decisions.label: Declared PFC 6.5 kA on UPS-output side
decisions.summary: Per IEC 62040-1:2017 Annex E typical for 10 kVA online double-conversion unit — significantly lower than utility-side 22.5 kA because the inverter limits short-circuit contribution.

sections.title: Circuit Breakdown
sections.summary: 6 final circuits arranged as 1 server rack (U01, 32A, 6 kVA 3-phase load) + 2 critical-workstation rings (U02 admin + U03 clinical, 16A each) + 1 lab-bench supply (U04, 10A) + 1 BMS/alarm panel (U05, 6A) + 1 UPS bypass maintenance loop (U06, 32A). U01 is the dominant critical load and the reason DB-UPS exists; U02-U04 are office-essential socket circuits backed by the UPS; U05 is a supervisory control circuit; U06 is the maintenance-bypass path.

decisions.label: All Type C MCBs
decisions.summary: Handles inrush of server-rack PSU energisation cluster + workstation cold-start + lab-bench inrush; Type B would risk nuisance trip during scheduled-maintenance restart.
decisions.rule: MCB curve selection for SMPS/server-rack loads

decisions.label: 32A for U01 server rack + U06 bypass; 16A workstation rings; 10A lab; 6A BMS
decisions.summary: U01 sized for 6 kVA 3-phase load (~9A per phase steady-state) with 3× headroom for boot-time cluster inrush; U06 mirrors U01 so the bypass can carry full load under maintenance changeover.

decisions.label: 1.5/2.5/6mm² LSZH Cu cabling
decisions.summary: LSZH for low toxic emission in server room; 6mm² for U01/U06 (32A capacity + voltage-drop margin); 2.5mm² for 10-16A circuits; 1.5mm² for BMS supervisory feed.

sections.title: Selectivity Analysis
sections.summary: Upstream MSB-MAIN F07 = 50A Type C MCB on the utility-side; UPS sits between MSB-MAIN F07 and DB-UPS; downstream MCBs at DB-UPS are 6-32A Type C. Worst-case ratio at U01/U06 (32A) = 50:32 = 1.56:1 — BELOW the 2:1 IEC 60898 same-curve cascade threshold for time-current selectivity. Cascade-critical: under a downstream U01 server-rack fault, both UPS internal protection and the upstream MSB-MAIN F07 may trip simultaneously, transferring the load to the bypass path. Verdict info — engineer-declared per UPS manufacturer cascade chart; fault-level skill to confirm at 6.5 kA UPS-output PFC + utility-side 22.5 kA + ride-through behaviour.

decisions.label: 50A:32A worst-case ratio = 1.56:1 at U01/U06
decisions.summary: BELOW the 2:1 IEC 60898 same-curve minimum cascade threshold — cascade-critical case requiring UPS manufacturer coordination chart verification.

decisions.label: UPS-output PFC 6.5 kA vs utility-side 22.5 kA
decisions.summary: UPS inverter limits short-circuit contribution; cascade study must account for both regimes (UPS-fed and bypass-fed fault paths).
decisions.rule: Cascade study with UPS in series

decisions.label: Defer to fault-level skill
decisions.summary: Manufacturer cascade table + UPS ride-through/bypass-changeover behaviour required at full coordination study; flagged INFO for fault-level skill invocation.

sections.title: Compliance Assessment
sections.summary: DB-UPS is compliant against IEC 60364 series (general LV distribution), IEC 62040-1:2017 (UPS general requirements + bypass + fault contribution), IEC 60364-5-53:2002+A2:2015 §531.3.3 (Type B RCD for server-rack IT loads), IEC 60364-4-41 §411.3.3 (Type A 30 mA RCD on socket circuits ≤32A), and IEEE 446-1995 (critical-power practice). Two INFO flags: (1) battery autonomy duration to be verified against UPS commissioning certificate at handover; (2) cascade ratio at U01/U06 (1.56:1) sits below 2:1 minimum — fault-level skill to confirm at full coordination study.

decisions.label: Type B RCD on U01 server rack
decisions.summary: Required by §531.3.3 — server-rack PDU/PSU/storage-controller SMPS produce smooth DC residual currents that blind Type A/F.
decisions.rule: RCD type selection for IT/server loads

decisions.label: Type A RCD on U02-U04 workstation + lab circuits
decisions.summary: Per §411.3.3 — 30 mA additional protection for socket circuits ≤32A; Type A covers pulsating-DC leakage from single-phase Class 1 PSUs.
decisions.rule: RCD type selection for general socket circuits

decisions.label: U05 BMS + U06 bypass RCD-free
decisions.summary: U05 is a supervisory control circuit with internal earth-leakage monitoring on the 24V DC field side; U06 is the UPS bypass requiring upstream fault-current flow per IEC 62040-1:2017 §5.2.4.

decisions.label: INFO flag — battery autonomy at handover
decisions.summary: Not a non-compliance; 30-min design intent must be verified against UPS commissioning certificate + battery discharge test report at handover.

decisions.label: INFO flag — cascade-critical at U01/U06
decisions.summary: Not a non-compliance; design flag noting 1.56:1 ratio below 2:1 minimum requires fault-level skill confirmation at 6.5 kA UPS-output PFC.

sections.title: Schedule Notes
sections.summary: Schedule emitted as 6-row table with 4 spare ways (W7-W10) for future expansion. Spare-way reservation aligns with typical UPS-load growth: additional server-rack zones, secondary BMS interface, video-wall/AV-control rack, or addressable PDU/environmental-monitoring sub-feed. Type B RCD flagged on U01 (server); Type A on U02-U04 (socket circuits); NO RCD on U05 (BMS) and U06 (bypass). Placard 'UPS-OUTPUT — DO NOT BACK-FEED' on faceplate.

decisions.label: 4 spare ways reserved
decisions.summary: Typical UPS-load expansion: additional server zones, secondary BMS interface, AV-control rack, or environmental monitoring.

decisions.label: Placard 'UPS-OUTPUT — DO NOT BACK-FEED'
decisions.summary: IEEE 446-1995 critical-power labelling practice — prevents maintainer cross-bonding UPS output to utility AC during fault investigation.

invariants.evidence: INV-14 board DB-UPS (INT → IEC): R1 jurisdiction→format mapping (INT/EU → IEC 60364-5-51 §514) holds; R2 board_label.text+svg populated, no {{}} remnants; R3 board_label.text matches IEC format regex; R4 6/6 circuits carry circuit_label.text+svg, no {{}} remnants; R5 every circuit_label.text matches IEC circuit regex; R6 tool_call_pending booleans set on all labels. Dual-source warning populated: "CAUTION: More than one source of supply (UPS)" (driver: UPS).

INV-15 board DB-UPS (INT): all 6 circuits carry diversity_basis with valid load_type enum + factor_applied in [0.0, 1.0] (Rule 1 PASS). All citations >=20 chars with recognisable clause markers (Reg / § / OSG / Table / CoP / TM) (Rule 2 PASS). Rule 3 N/A (no lift or ev_charger circuits on this board). method_params present where method='largest_plus_remainder_pct' (Rule 4 PASS).

board_label.text: DB-UPS | U=400 V TPN+E | I_n=50 A | CAUTION: More than one source of supply (UPS)
